expectations.text: Adds TimeGenerated filter that was missing in the original
expectations.evidence: Both the optimized query and optimization-notes.md explicitly add TimeGenerated: '| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)' in SecurityEvent and SigninLogs subquery. Notes section title: '1. Missing Time Filter (Critical — Full Table Scan)'

optimization-notes.md section '2. Unnecessary tolower() (Performance)' explains removal. The optimized query still uses tolower() but only for username extraction from Account/UPN format (a different purpose), not for the case-insensitive has comparison. The original tolower() on the has/contains comparison is gone.

expectations.text: Replaces contains with has or =~ for the admin string match
expectations.evidence: '| where Account has adminKeyword' in the query. Notes section '3. contains Instead of has (Performance — ~10x slower)' explains the change.

expectations.text: Filters both sides of the join before joining (adds where to SigninLogs subquery)
expectations.evidence: SigninLogs subquery includes '| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)' before being joined. Notes section '4. Join Without Pre-filtering SigninLogs' documents this.

expectations.text: Provides explanation of each optimization and why it improves performance
expectations.evidence: optimization-notes.md contains 7 numbered sections, each with Problem/Fix explanations covering: time filter, tolower removal, has vs contains, join pre-filtering, join key mismatch, column projection, and join hint

expectations.text: Adds project to reduce column transfer before join or summarize
expectations.evidence: '| project TimeGenerated, Account, Computer' before summarize in SecurityEvent pipeline. Notes section '6. No Column Projection Before Join' documents this. SigninLogs side also uses '| project UserPrincipalName, SigninTime = TimeGenerated, ResultType, AppDisplayName, IPAddress'

claims.claim: Identifies and fixes the join key mismatch between DOMAIN\username and UPN format
claims.evidence: Notes section 5 details 'Join Key Mismatch — Logic Bug' and the query uses split(Account, '\\') and split(UserPrincipalName, '@') to extract comparable username portions

claims.claim: Adds hint.strategy = shuffle to the join
claims.evidence: '| join hint.strategy = shuffle kind = inner (...) on AccountUsername'

suggestions.reason: The join key mismatch fix (DOMAIN\user vs UPN) is arguably the most impactful correctness improvement and no assertion checks for it. Consider adding an assertion: 'Identifies or fixes the join key mismatch between SecurityEvent.Account (DOMAIN\user) and SigninLogs.UserPrincipalName (UPN) formats'.

eval_feedback.overall: Strong output — all assertions pass with clear evidence. The join key mismatch fix is a significant correctness improvement that goes unchecked by any assertion.
